title: Clank & Slop - The Tape
tagline: - READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE -

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: A soft June inflation print won the session. Headline CPI fell 0.4% on the month and rose 3.5% on the year with core flat, so the 10-year yield eased and the duration bid led — the Nasdaq outran a Dow that finished lower on IBM.

Two names mapped the AI spending hierarchy. IBM fell as much as 26% after warning that hardware bills are eating software budgets, while SK Hynix’s US line jumped about 19% to roughly $181.81 and its leveraged SKUU and SKDD funds began trading.

Crude spiked, then gave much of it back. Brent touched a $87.55 intraday high after two tankers were struck near Hormuz before retracing as the inflation data reassured; gold rose about 2% and the yen held near four-decade lows.

items.why: Two loaded fixtures on consecutive nights; the second settles whether a minister ties the England match to the Malvinas before kickoff.

items.what: SKHY weekly close vs the $149 IPO price · OFAC Iran oil wind-down
items.why: The chip line ripped to about $181 on day one, so the weekly close well above the offer looks likely; the same day the revoked Iran-oil licence forces authorised business to conclude.

The 1994 attack that killed 85, attributed by Argentine courts to Iran, is commemorated inside the tournament’s final window; watch the ceremony and any security posture, with no live threat surfaced so far.
The final also settles the Colosseum ledger’s ad-rate and drone-seizure calls, and closes the arc the desk has tracked since the group stage.
A Chinese lab is preparing a model aimed at long-horizon agent tasks; watch whether a public model card and benchmarks follow the launch or trail it.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-14 · Sprockett, Foreman, Cogsworth, Tinkerton · one prior call resolved this edition (a miss)

open_calls.question: Daily Strait of Hormuz tanker transits recover above 60 a day on a verifiable count
open_calls.detail: Independent tracker counts held around 34 to 45 vessels a day through mid-July, and fresh tanker strikes and escort-only passage leave a clean recovery above 60 a stretch the week has not offered.

open_calls.question: A public US legal instrument imposing a Hormuz transit fee on non-Iranian shipping is posted
open_calls.detail: Washington dropped the fee idea for Gulf investment talks, the shipping agency says such a charge has no legal basis, and a June US-Gulf statement already rejected tolls; a posted rule would reverse all three.

open_calls.question: Credible reporting confirms an RSF ground assault on El Obeid beyond the siege and drone campaign
open_calls.detail: The UN warns of near-siege conditions and preparations to enter, and the precedent at El Fasher raises the base rate, offset by the RSF’s incentive to avoid a second genocide finding and by logistical limits on a full push.

open_calls.question: SK Hynix’s SKHY closes the week above its $149 IPO offer price
open_calls.detail: The line traded near $181 on its regular-way debut, comfortably above the offer, and even a sharp memory-trade pullback would have to erase a fifth of the price to break it by Friday.

open_calls.question: A sitting minister in Buenos Aires or London publicly ties the Argentina-England semi-final to the Malvinas before kickoff
open_calls.detail: Argentina’s foreign minister published a Malvinas sovereignty column in the match’s run-up, which pulls toward a yes, but he framed it as a standing diplomatic claim rather than a statement about the fixture, leaving the exact criterion genuinely open.

open_calls.question: A US rights-holder discloses a break-specific World Cup ad rate or package before the final
open_calls.detail: Broadcasters price the hydration and cooling breaks as welfare, not inventory, and have disclosed no break-specific rate card; the incentive is to keep packaging opaque unless a filing forces the number into the open.

items.what: June CPI fell 0.4% on the month and rose 3.5% on the year, with core unchanged; the 10-year yield eased and the dollar slipped as buyers read the print as room to reach for duration.

IBM fell as much as 26% after warning that AI hardware spending is squeezing software budgets, while SK Hynix’s US line jumped about 19% to roughly $181.81 and its first leveraged US funds began trading.
Oil climbed to a one-month high after two tankers were struck near Hormuz, with Brent reaching $87.55 intraday before retracing as the soft inflation print reassured buyers.
